Family Titles and Teams

Family titles are titles that are all released by the same publisher. Titles in the family all share the same family title ID. You must include the family title ID in the certificate of each title in the family. In addition, each title in the family has its own unique ID that also must appear in the certificate.

Using family title IDs with teams enables team members to play as a team across more than one of your titles. Suppose, for instance, you are releasing a family of sports games. If you use family title IDs, a given team can play any sport in the family of titles without having to recreate their team for each title.

If your family title ID appears in the certificates of your games on Xbox Live, your program can use the family title ID by calling the XOnlineTeamSetFamilyTitleID function. It must call this function before it creates a team, enumerates teams, or calls any other XOnlineTeam functions. After your program calls XOnlineTeamSetFamilyTitleID, all of the function XOnlineTeam function calls use the family title ID instead of normal title ID.

After calling XOnlineTeamSetFamilyTitleID, titles should pass the team family title ID to XOnlineMessageEnableReceivingFamilyTitleIDs in order to receive notifications of incoming XONLINE_MSG_TYPE_TEAM_RECRUIT messages via XOnlineGetNotificationEx and to have those messages returned by XOnlineMessageEnumerate.

Note    Family title IDs can be uses for other features besides teams. They are enabled on a feature-by-feature basis using the appropriate APIs. A game may use up to 4 family title IDs. As a result, you do not have to implement family title IDs across all features. For example, if you want to enable messaging across several of your titles using a family title ID, you are not required to also share storage and teams across those titles.
